$schema_note: Iter-9 backfill snapshot fixture (the 4 us-ecommerce-* fixtures were referenced by us-ecommerce.json since Iter-7 but were never on disk; only us-services-* existed). Source-of-record for demand_volatility inputs cited by domestic ecommerce profiles. The profile's source.ref points here; profile inputs MIRROR this file's data block.
applies_to_note: US ecommerce / consumer-retail sector. pmi_current=47.0 indicates moderate contraction in consumer-facing demand (below the 50.0 expansion/contraction threshold).

source.citation: Synthetic Iter-7 fixture (pmi_current=47.0 moderate contraction; pmi_neutral=50.0 PMI convention). Formula spec per design-v1-risk-factor-catalog.md b.1.
source.retrieval_method: fabricated for the Iter-7 composer test; no live pipeline. STAYS snapshot in Iter-9 (pmi_current has no free API: ISM commercial; S&P/au Jibun release-day-free, no REST API; no FRED PMI series).
source.live_binding_target: NONE via direct bind. Would require reformulating demand_volatility onto a FRED ECOMPCTSA / Census E-commerce Retail signal (separate later iteration).

_meta.iteration_authored: Iter-9 (backfill; values mirror us-ecommerce.json Iter-7 inline inputs)
